parsed.clarification_question: Do you want (A) sentence-level copyediting only (clarity, grammar, concision, no content changes), or (B) permission to also adjust content for internal consistency and claim strength (e.g., reconcile the contradiction about adaptation under 4-hour replanning and soften "first principled approach" language)?

rationale.R1.2: The passage contains an apparent contradiction about calibration breaking versus automatic online adaptation, so logical consistency may need attention during cleanup.
rationale.R1.6: "Clean this up" principally implies polishing wording, grammar, and flow at the sentence/paragraph level.
rationale.RX.1: When rephrasing empirical and capability claims, uncertainty should be preserved or explicitly qualified.
rationale.RX.2: Strong novelty and capability assertions require careful phrasing to avoid overclaiming during cleanup.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay within the requested cleanup scope and avoid adding new analysis or claims.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should avoid creating misleading guidance in a safety-relevant scheduling context.
rationale.RX.5: Revisions must maintain traceability to the original statements and avoid introducing unsubstantiated content.
